full_response:
Step-1 :  Write out the double sum  

  η_{i\mu}\,η^{\mu i} ≡  Σ_{i=1}^{3} Σ_{\mu =0}^{3} η_{i\mu}\,η^{\mu i}.

(The index i is restricted to the spatial values 1,2,3, while the index μ runs over 0,1,2,3.)

Step-2 :  Use the fact that the Minkowski metric is diagonal  

  η_{00}=-1, η_{11}=η_{22}=η_{33}=+1, η_{αβ}=0  for α≠β.

Because the metric is diagonal, η_{i\mu} is non-zero only when μ=i.  
Hence the inner sum collapses to μ=i:

  η_{i\mu}\,η^{\mu i}=η_{ii}\,η^{ii}. 

Step-3 :  Evaluate each term.

For any spatial index i=1,2,3,

  η_{ii}=+1  and η^{ii}=+1,

so η_{ii}η^{ii}=1.

Step-4 :  Add over the three spatial indices.

  η_{i\mu}η^{\mu i}= Σ_{i=1}^{3} 1 = 3.

Step-5 :  Decide whether it is a Lorentz scalar.

All indices that appear are summed over (each repeated once up and once down),
so the resulting quantity is a rank-0 tensor—a scalar.
Because the only object used is the Minkowski metric, whose components remain
η_{μν}=diag(-1,1,1,1) in every inertial frame, the value 3 is the same in all Lorentz frames.
Therefore the result is indeed a Lorentz scalar (equal to the number of spatial dimensions).

\boxed{\,\eta_{i\mu}\,\eta^{\mu i}=3\;}
\boxed{\text{Yes, this is a Lorentz scalar.}}

Confidence: 65%
